title: 10 best herbal essential oils for ringworm
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for ringworm are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate symptoms and prevent the spread of the fungal infection. These oils contain antimicrobial properties that combat the fungus causing ringworm, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications. The best essential oils for ringworm include Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Cymbopogon citratus, Azadirachta indica, Thymus vulgaris, Lavandula angustifolia, Syzygium aromaticum, Allium sativum, Origanum vulgare, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, ringworm can lead to emotional distress, social isolation, and permanent scarring, significantly impacting a person's quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which allow it to target and eliminate fungal infections that cause this common skin condition. The oil's active compound, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, a type of fungus responsible for many cases of ringworm. Additionally, tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe itchy and irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healing. Its antimicrobial properties also work to prevent further infection and promote healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Tea Tree Essential Oil has potent antifungal activity, which makes it effective against fungal infections like ringworm.
Antibacterial property: The oil's antibacterial properties can help combat secondary bacterial infections that often accompany ringworm and other fungal conditions.
Anti-inflammatory property: Tea Tree Essential Oil may reduce inflammation, redness, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and improving the overall symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the tea tree plant are rich in antimicrobial compounds, making them an effective choice for treating fungal infections like ringworm.
Seeds: Tea tree seeds contain a high concentration of terpinen-4-ol, a compound that has been shown to have antifungal properties and can help combat ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of calendula officinalis at dawn and dry them in a warm, well-ventilated area for two days.
Combine 20 grams of dried calendula officinalis with 10 grams of dried chamomilla recutita and mix well to create a uniform blend.
Add 5 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil to the blend and stir until it forms a smooth paste.
Infuse the blend in a double boiler setup for one hour, then strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Bottle the final product and store it in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil essential oils helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which allow it to effectively target and eliminate fungal infections that cause this condition. The oil's natural compounds, such as eugenol and linalool, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida and other pathogenic microorganisms responsible for ringworm. Additionally, basil essential oils' anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal basil essential oil exhibits antimicrobial activity, which helps to combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth and spread of pathogens.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Basil's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antifungal properties: The antifungal compounds in basil essential oil have been shown to exhibit significant activity against various fungal species, including those that cause ringworm, making it a potent natural treatment option.
remedy_parts: Roots: Basil roots contain anti-fungal compounds that help combat ringworm infection and promote skin healing.
Barks: The barks of the basil plant have been traditionally used to treat fungal infections, including ringworm, due to their antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh basil leaves ocimum basilicum from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Dry the harvested basil leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 6 hours or air-drying for 24 hours.
Steam distill 200g of dried basil leaves in a stainless steel still with 1l of distilled water at 100°c for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dry place.
Dilute the ocimum basilicum essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a 3% concentration for topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal and antibacterial properties. The oil contains compounds such as citral and geraniol, which have been shown to effectively combat fungal infections like ringworm, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, lemongrass essential oils have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe itchy skin, reduce redness, and prevent further infection, making it a popular natural remedy for treating ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antifungal: Lemongrass essential oil exhibits antifungal properties, which allow it to effectively combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth and reproduction of fungi.

Antiseptic: The antimicrobial properties of lemongrass essential oil also make it effective against bacterial and viral infections, helping to prevent secondary infections that can complicate ringworm treatment.

Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, making it more comfortable to manage the infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against fungal infections like ringworm.
Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, making them useful for treating ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning after the dew has dried.
Steam distill 200g of harvested leaves for 4 hours using a glass apparatus.
Collect and filter the extracted oil through a coffee filter paper.
Mix 10ml of distilled oil with 20ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Store the final blend in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause ringworm. Additionally, ginger essential oil has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and calm the affected skin, reducing redness and itching associated with ringworm. Its natural antibacterial properties also aid in preventing secondary infections, making it a effective and holistic treatment for ringworm.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, making it more comfortable to treat.
Antifungal: Ginger essential oil exhibits antifungal properties, which combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm, inhibiting its growth and preventing further spread.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of ginger essential oil helps kill bacteria and other microorganisms that may contribute to ringworm infections, promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is a thick, underground stem that is rich in compounds that have anti-fungal and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating ringworm infections.
Barks: The barks of the ginger plant contain volatile oils that have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in ringworm cases.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger roots weighing 200 grams.
Dry the ginger roots in shade for 14 days.
Steam distill the dried ginger roots at 100°c for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter through paper coffee filters.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottle with tight lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which effectively combat the fungal infection that causes this common skin condition. The oil's active compounds, such as caryophyllene and alpha-pinene, work together to inhibit fungal growth, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oils' antimicrobial properties help to eliminate the ringworm fungus from the affected area, promoting a healthy and rapid recovery.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil's antifungal property helps combat fungal infections like ringworm by inhibiting the growth and reproduction of fungi, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, allowing the skin to heal faster and more effectively.

Antioxidant activity: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin damage caused by ringworm, promoting healthy tissue regeneration and wound repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which exhibit antifungal properties and can help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Rhyzomes: High in anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce redness and swelling associated with ringworm lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eucalyptus globulus leaves in large quantities from a trusted source, ideally fresh and free of pesticides.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or air-drying for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with distilled water in a ratio of 1:4, monitoring temperature and pressure.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil through cheesecloth or paper filters, storing it in a dark glass bottle.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 24 hours before use, shaking the bottle occasionally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties, which allow it to effectively combat fungal infections like ringworm. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing also contributes to its beneficial effects. Additionally, thyme essential oil's antimicrobial properties help to prevent the spread of the infection by eliminating any bacteria or other microorganisms that may be contributing to the condition. This makes thyme essential oil a popular natural remedy for treating ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Thyme essential oil's antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections such as ringworm, by inhibiting the growth and spread of fungal cells.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory properties of thyme essential oil can reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery and reducing discomfort.

Antibacterial action: Thyme essential oil's antibacterial properties help to prevent secondary bacterial infections that can occur when the skin is compromised by ringworm, further complicating the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are good for making medicinal essential oils for ringworm because they contain a high concentration of antimicrobial compounds that help combat fungal infections.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are useful for making medicinal essential oils for ringworm due to their rich content of flavonoids, which possess antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn or early morning for optimal oil quality.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloth for 24 hours.
Steam distill 500g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 1 liter of water for 4-6 hours to produce essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or coffee filter into a dark glass bottle using a dropper.
Store the thymus vulgaris essential oil in a cool, dark place at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which make it an effective natural remedy to combat the fungal infection. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing also aids in speeding up the recovery process. Additionally, rosemary essential oil's antiseptic properties help prevent further infection and promote a healthy environment for the affected area, making it an excellent alternative or complement to traditional treatments for ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: Rosemary essential oil has antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungal infections like ringworm, helping to reduce its spread and severity.

Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, promoting a faster recovery and minimizing discomfort.
Antioxidant property: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate ringworm symptoms and prolong healing time.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain antimicrobial compounds that help combat fungal infections like ringworm.
Barks: Rosemary barks have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe itchy and inflamed skin, a common symptom of ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves on a sunny day in the morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least 24 hours to reduce moisture.
Steam distill the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves using 100ml of water for every 20g of herbs over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Collect the resulting essential oil by condensing the steam vapor into a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place immediately.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 24 hours before using or storing it in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pigeon pea essential oils helps with ringworm because of its potent antifungal properties. The oil contains compounds like cajanol, which has been shown to exhibit significant inhibition against various fungal strains, including those responsible for ringworm infections. When applied topically, the oil's antifungal agents work to reduce inflammation, soothe itching and burning sensations, and promote healthy skin regeneration. Additionally, the oil's antimicrobial properties help prevent secondary bacterial infections, accelerating the healing process and promoting a faster recovery from ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: The methanol extract of Cajanus cajan has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties against various fungal species, including Candida albicans, Aspergillus niger, and Trichophyton rubrum, which are known to cause ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory activity: The extract has also been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm infections.
Wound healing properties: Cajanus cajan has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to accelerate wound healing, including skin lesions caused by ringworm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, pigeon pea roots have been traditionally used to treat fungal infections like ringworm due to their antifungal properties.
Rhyzomes: The underground stem of the pigeon pea plant contains a high concentration of saponins and flavonoids, making it effective against ringworm-causing fungi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cajanus cajan leaves measure 10 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves in shade for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a clevenger apparatus.
Collect and filter the extracted essential oil into a clean glass vial.
Store the herbal essential oil in an amber bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which effectively combat the fungal infection that causes ringworm. The aloe vera plant has been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including fungal infections. The essential oil extracted from the plant contains compounds like aloin and acemannan, which have potent antifungal activity. When applied topically, these compounds can penetrate deep into the skin, killing the fungus that causes ringworm and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_properties: Antifungal and antiseptic properties: Aloe vera's active compounds, such as acemannan and aloin, help to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, promoting a healthy environment for skin recovery.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera reduces inflammation and swelling associated with ringworm, allowing the affected area to heal more quickly and reducing discomfort.
Soothing and moisturizing properties: Aloe vera's gel soothes and calms irritated skin, helping to reduce redness and itching, while also providing hydration to promote healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them effective in treating fungal infections like ringworm.
Barks: The barks of the aloe plant contain antimicrobial agents that help to prevent the growth of fungi and bacteria, which can contribute to the development of ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera leaves from mature plants by cutting them at soil level using sterile scissors or pruning shears.
Remove the spines and pulp from the harvested aloe vera leaves using a sharp knife or vegetable peeler.
Chop the de-spined aloe vera leaves into small pieces and mix with 1 part coconut oil for a 3-5 ratio blend.
Steam distill the aloe vera and coconut oil mixture in an essential oil extractor for 2-3 hours to extract oils.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and debris.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with ringworm because of its antifungal properties, which effectively combat fungal infections. The oil's active compounds, such as calendula and lutein, have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including ringworm. These compounds work by suppressing the growth of fungus, reducing inflammation, and promoting healthy skin cell regeneration. remedy_properties: Antifungal: Marigold essential oil's antifungal property helps to combat fungal infections like Ringworm, preventing its spread and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory: Marigold essential oil's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling, redness, and itching associated with Ringworm, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Antimicrobial: Marigold essential oil's antimicrobial property inhibits the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to Ringworm infections, promoting a healthy environment for healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Pot Marigold plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and antibacterial compounds, making them effective in treating fungal infections like ringworm.
Buds: The buds of the Pot Marigold plant contain a high amount of flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to have antifungal properties that can help combat ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom ensuring they are completely free of pests and diseases.
Dry the fresh calendula flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried calendula flowers in a still using 20g of flowers per 100ml of distilled water for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting oil through cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Allow the oil to mature for at least 24 hours before use to allow for the essential compounds to settle.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ringworm with herbal essential oils is to mix a few drops of tea tree oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply the blend topically three times a day to the affected area for several weeks until the infection clears up. Other essential oils like lavender, oregano, and lemongrass have also shown antimicrobial properties that can help combat ringworm.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ringworm that are treated with herbal essential oils are fungal infections such as athlete's foot, jock itch, and nail fungus. The antifungal properties of herbs like tea tree oil, oregano oil, and lavender oil help to combat these conditions by reducing inflammation, killing fungal spores, and promoting healthy skin and nails.
